Operation 'login' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too minimal. Added clarity about credential requirements, token return value, how to use the token, and session establishment side effect for better AI agent understanding of authentication flow.

data.description_suggestion: Authenticates a user with credentials (username/password) and returns an authentication token for subsequent API requests. The returned token should be used in the Authorization header for authenticated operations. Establishes a user session.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added clarity about credential requirements, token return value, how to use the token, and session establishment side effect for better AI agent understanding of authentication flow.

message: Operation 'logout' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks context about authentication requirement and side effects. Added information about token invalidation, session clearing, and that this operation requires prior authentication for AI agents to understand the logout flow.

data.description_suggestion: Logs out the authenticated user and invalidates their authentication token. Requires valid authentication. Clears the user session and revokes token access for subsequent API requests.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks context about authentication requirement and side effects. Added information about token invalidation, session clearing, and that this operation requires prior authentication for AI agents to understand the logout flow.

message: Operation 'getCurrentUser' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description is too generic. Added authentication requirement context and clarified what User object properties are returned to help AI agents understand the operation's purpose and output.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the profile information of the currently authenticated user. Requires valid authentication token. Returns the User object containing user ID, username, email, and other profile attributes.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic. Added authentication requirement context and clarified what User object properties are returned to help AI agents understand the operation's purpose and output.

message: Operation 'createCertificate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is minimal and lacks domain context. Added information about certificate types supported, input schema reference, return value, and side effect of immediate availability. This helps AI agents understand what types of certificates can be created and what to expect.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new certificate (Root CA, Intermediate CA, or end-entity certificate) in the PKI infrastructure. Accepts CertificateCreate payload with certificate type, subject details, and validity parameters. Returns the created Certificate object with assigned ID and metadata. The certificate is immediately available for use.
data.reasoning: Original description is minimal and lacks domain context. Added information about certificate types supported, input schema reference, return value, and side effect of immediate availability. This helps AI agents understand what types of certificates can be created and what to expect.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed information for a specific certificate by ID. Requires the certificate ID as a path parameter. Returns the Certificate object containing certificate metadata, validity dates, subject information, and other certificate properties.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about input requirements and output content. Added clarity about the ID parameter requirement and what Certificate object properties are returned to improve AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'updateCertificate' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too vague. Added context about PATCH semantics (partial updates), ID parameter requirement, input schema reference, return value, and clarification that only specified fields are modified. This helps AI agents understand the update behavior.

data.description_suggestion: Updates specific properties of an existing certificate using partial update (PATCH). Requires the certificate ID as a path parameter and CertificateUpdate payload with fields to modify. Returns the updated Certificate object. Only provided fields are modified; other properties remain unchanged.
data.reasoning: Original description is too vague. Added context about PATCH semantics (partial updates), ID parameter requirement, input schema reference, return value, and clarification that only specified fields are modified. This helps AI agents understand the update behavior.

message: Operation 'deleteCertificate' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is extremely vague with no context about what happens during deletion, authentication requirements, or side effects. Added clarity about permanence, authentication, potential cascading effects on dependent certificates, and expected response code for better AI agent decision-making.

data.summary_suggestion: Delete a certificate from the system
data.description_suggestion: Permanently removes a certificate from the PKI system. Requires authentication. This operation deletes the certificate record and associated metadata. Note: Deleting a CA certificate may affect dependent certificates. Returns 204 No Content on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: Original description is extremely vague with no context about what happens during deletion, authentication requirements, or side effects. Added clarity about permanence, authentication, potential cascading effects on dependent certificates, and expected response code for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'revokeCertificate' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description lacks all essential context. Added distinction between revocation and deletion, authentication requirements, input expectations (revocation reason), output details, CRL implications, and irreversibility to help AI agents understand the operation's significance and proper usage.

data.description_suggestion: Marks a certificate as revoked in the PKI system without deleting it. Requires authentication and a revocation reason. The revoked certificate will be included in future Certificate Revocation Lists (CRLs). Returns the updated certificate object with revocation status and timestamp. This operation is irreversible.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks all essential context. Added distinction between revocation and deletion, authentication requirements, input expectations (revocation reason), output details, CRL implications, and irreversibility to help AI agents understand the operation's significance and proper usage.

message: Operation 'getCRL' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs context about what the CRL contains, output format, authentication requirements, and use case to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current Certificate Revocation List (CRL) for the specified CA certificate. The CRL contains all revoked certificates issued by this CA. Returns the CRL in PEM format by default. No authentication required for CRL retrieval (standard PKI practice). Useful for clients validating certificate status.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs context about what the CRL contains, output format, authentication requirements, and use case to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

message: Operation 'generateCRL' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is too minimal. Added context about what gets included in the CRL, authentication requirements, side effects (timestamp updates), output format, and operational guidance for AI agents to understand when and why to trigger CRL generation.

data.summary_suggestion: Generate an updated Certificate Revocation List
data.description_suggestion: Generates a new Certificate Revocation List (CRL) for the specified CA certificate, incorporating all currently revoked certificates. Requires authentication. Updates the CRL's nextUpdate timestamp. Returns the newly generated CRL in PEM format. This operation should be performed periodically or after certificate revocations to ensure clients have current revocation information.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added context about what gets included in the CRL, authentication requirements, side effects (timestamp updates), output format, and operational guidance for AI agents to understand when and why to trigger CRL generation.

message: Operation 'renewCertificate' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description provides no context about the renewal process. Added clarity about what renewal means in PKI context (new certificate with extended validity), authentication requirements, what happens to the original certificate, output details, and key preservation to help AI agents understand the operation's mechanics and effects.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new certificate with the same subject and parameters as the existing certificate, extending its validity period. Requires authentication. The original certificate remains valid until expiration. Returns the newly issued certificate object with updated validity dates and serial number. The renewal process preserves the certificate's key pair and subject information.
data.reasoning: Original description provides no context about the renewal process. Added clarity about what renewal means in PKI context (new certificate with extended validity), authentication requirements, what happens to the original certificate, output details, and key preservation to help AI agents understand the operation's mechanics and effects.
